detaileddescription.para: Compares 2 Bluetooth UUIDs, if the types are different both UUIDs are first converted to 128 bits format before comparing.

parameterdescription.para: First Bluetooth UUID to compare
Second Bluetooth UUID to compare

simplesect.para: negative value if u1 < u2, 0 if u1 == u2, else positive

briefdescription.para: Create a bt_uuid from a little-endian data buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Create a bt_uuid from a little-endian data buffer. The data_len parameter is used to determine whether the UUID is in 16, 32 or 128 bit format (length 2, 4 or 16). Note: 32 bit format is not allowed over the air.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the bt_uuid variable
pointer to UUID stored in little-endian data buffer
length of the UUID in the data buffer

simplesect.para: true if the data was valid and the UUID was successfully created.

detaileddescription.para: Converts Bluetooth UUID to string. UUID can be in any format, 16-bit, 32-bit or 128-bit.

parameterdescription.para: Bluetooth UUID
pointer where to put converted string
length of str

briefdescription.para: Convert a UUID string to a Bluetooth UUID.

detaileddescription.para: Parses a UUID string and stores the result in a bt_uuid_any. The UUID type (16/32/128-bit) is determined by the input string length.

Accepted formats:
4 hex digits (e.g. "180D") -> BT_UUID_TYPE_16
8 hex digits (e.g. "0000180D") -> BT_UUID_TYPE_32
36-char canonical form (e.g. "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx") -> BT_UUID_TYPE_128
A full 128-bit UUID string always produces BT_UUID_TYPE_128, even if it matches the Bluetooth Base UUID pattern. Use the short forms (4 or 8 hex chars) to obtain 16-bit or 32-bit types.

Notes:
No "0x" prefix.
No leading/trailing whitespace.
Only hexadecimal characters (case-insensitive).
128-bit UUIDs must include hyphens in the standard positions.
16/32-bit UUIDs must not include separators or hyphens.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the UUID string.
Pointer to a bt_uuid_any to receive the result.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or a negative error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Compress a 128-bit UUID to 16-bit or 32-bit if it matches the Bluetooth Base UUID.

detaileddescription.para: If src is already 16-bit or 32-bit it is copied as-is. A 128-bit UUID whose bytes [4..15] match the Bluetooth Base UUID is compressed to 16-bit (when bytes [0..1] of the RFC 9562 form are zero) or 32-bit. Non-matching 128-bit UUIDs are not compressed and cause the function to return -ENOTSUP without modifying dst.

parameterdescription.para: Source UUID to compress.
Destination to receive the (possibly shorter) UUID.
UUID was compressed (or copied for 16/32-bit input).
128-bit UUID does not match the Bluetooth Base UUID; dst is left unmodified.

briefdescription.para: Size in octets of a 16-bit UUID.
Size in octets of a 32-bit UUID.
Size in octets of a 128-bit UUID.

parameterdescription.para: 16-bit UUID value in host endianness.
32-bit UUID value in host endianness.
128-bit UUID array values in little-endian format. Can be combined with BT_UUID_128_ENCODE to initialize a UUID from the readable form of UUIDs.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare a 16-bit UUID inline.

parameterdescription.para: 16-bit UUID value in host endianness.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare a 32-bit UUID inline.

parameterdescription.para: 32-bit UUID value in host endianness.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare a 128-bit UUID inline.

parameterdescription.para: 128-bit UUID array values in little-endian format. Can be combined with BT_UUID_128_ENCODE to declare a UUID from the readable form of UUIDs.

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to access the 16-bit UUID from a generic UUID.
Helper macro to access the 32-bit UUID from a generic UUID.
Helper macro to access the 128-bit UUID from a generic UUID.
Encode 128 bit UUID into array values in little-endian format.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to initialize a 128-bit UUID array value from the readable form of UUIDs, or encode 128-bit UUID values into advertising data Can be combined with BT_UUID_DECLARE_128 to declare a 128-bit UUID.
Example of how to declare the UUID 6E400001-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E
Example of how to encode the UUID 6E400001-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E into advertising data.
Just replace the hyphen by the comma and add 0x prefixes.

parameterdescription.para: First part of the UUID (32 bits)
Second part of the UUID (16 bits)
Third part of the UUID (16 bits)
Fourth part of the UUID (16 bits)
Fifth part of the UUID (48 bits)

simplesect.para: The comma separated values for UUID 128 initializer that may be used directly as an argument for BT_UUID_INIT_128 or BT_UUID_DECLARE_128

briefdescription.para: Encode 16-bit UUID into array values in little-endian format.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to encode 16-bit UUID values into advertising data.
Example of how to encode the UUID 0x180a into advertising data.

parameterdescription.para: UUID value (16-bits)

simplesect.para: The comma separated values for UUID 16 value that may be used directly as an argument for BT_DATA_BYTES.

briefdescription.para: Encode 32-bit UUID into array values in little-endian format.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to encode 32-bit UUID values into advertising data.
Example of how to encode the UUID 0x180a01af into advertising data.

parameterdescription.para: UUID value (32-bits)

simplesect.para: The comma separated values for UUID 32 value that may be used directly as an argument for BT_DATA_BYTES.

briefdescription.para: Recommended length of user string buffer for Bluetooth UUID.

detaileddescription.para: The recommended length guarantee the output of UUID conversion will not lose valuable information about the UUID being processed. If the length of the UUID is known the string can be shorter.
